reason_for_decision: The revised article clears the pass threshold with clearer capability boundaries, stronger layered-system framing, and a disciplined technical tone.
strengths: The article gives beginners a stable mental model by defining RF detection as listening for transmitters rather than searching for physical objects.
It now distinguishes detection, decoding, and identification instead of flattening all RF capability into one vague category.
The limits around silent targets, dense spectrum, and location ambiguity are stated clearly enough to keep the piece technically credible.
summary_assessment: This version is publishable under the stricter rubric. It is specific, restrained, and useful for readers trying to understand where RF detection fits in a layered sensing stack.
